content.problemStatement:
Chef decided to make a big picnic. He chose a beautiful place for this special event. Then, Chef placed NNN picnic baskets on the ground at some points. The only thing remaining is placing some picnic blankets underneath the baskets in such a way that each basket lies on at least one blanket (the blankets are allowed to overlap and each basket may lie on more than one blanket).

Each picnic blanket can be represented as an axis-aligned square. Chef may place any number of blankets on the ground, arbitrarily and independently choosing the side length of each blanket. However, for each blanket, there must be at least three baskets lying on it.

Your task is to help Chef with the preparations by minimizing the size of the largest blanket.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. Each test case starts with a line containing a single integer N denoting the number of baskets. Each of the following N lines contains two space-separated integers X and Y denoting the coordinates of one basket.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the minimum possible side length of the largest blanket (the blanket with the maximum side length).

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: Example case 1: Chef can place two picnic blankets on the ground. The first blanket has two opposite corners at points (1,1) and (2,2), the second blanket at points (1,2) and (4,5). Then, the largest blanket (blanket 2) has side length 3. Example case 2: Chef can place one picnic blanket on the ground. The blanket has two opposite corners at points (1,1) and (1,43). Then, the largest blanket has side length 42.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a brute force approach. For each pair of baskets, we can calculate the side length of the blanket that covers these two baskets and at least one more basket. We keep track of the minimum side length of the largest blanket found so far.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^3) — for each test case, we iterate over all pairs of baskets and for each pair, we check all other baskets to find the minimum side length of the largest blanket.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — we need to store the coordinates of all baskets.
